existing_capability: Records security- and privacy-relevant events with agents purposes policies outcomes sources and entities
route_c_use: Structured projection of the disclosure or access event
route_c_profile_decision: Profile the minimum actors source recipient purpose outcome Consent and versioned target semantics required by the frozen hero case
explicit_nonclaim: TrustEvidence is not a new AuditEvent resource and does not make FHIR audit logs complete
evidence_required: C3 positive and negative examples; SUSHI; IG Publisher; HL7 Validator; mapping audit

existing_capability: Records activities agents entities and version-specific targets involved in producing revising signing importing or transforming a resource
route_c_use: Represents source-to-portable-evidence transformation and referenced clinical-resource version
route_c_profile_decision: Use Provenance only for origin and transformation semantics; do not substitute it for an access AuditEvent or issuer signature
explicit_nonclaim: Provenance metadata does not by itself prove the truth of the source clinical event
evidence_required: C3 profile and examples; version-reference tests; semantic round trip

existing_capability: Represents consumer choices permissions denials recipients purposes periods policy context and partial derivatives of a source directive
route_c_use: Provides the versioned Consent resource or reference against which the event-time decision is described
route_c_profile_decision: Keep Consent state policy and authorisation outcome distinct; transport only the minimum authorised derivative or reference
explicit_nonclaim: A Consent status is not automatically an access-control decision and no legal-validity claim is made
evidence_required: C3 consent instance; mapping tests; consent-version mutation in C4

existing_capability: Defines reusable FHIR R4 AuditEvent patterns for REST activities token use consent-authorised decisions and privacy disclosures
route_c_use: Provides the closest published pattern for the FHIR-facing disclosure event
route_c_profile_decision: Derive from or validate against only the profiles actually applicable to the frozen case; otherwise publish an explicit mapping matrix
explicit_nonclaim: No IHE certification and no BALP conformance wording until the exact examples pass the declared package and validator toolchain
evidence_required: C3 package-pinned validation and profile-combination report

route_c_use: Canonicalises the TrustEvidence payload before issuer signing
route_c_profile_decision: Use the installed RFC 8785 implementation under a declared application profile and reject unsupported values before signing
explicit_nonclaim: TrustEvidence does not define a new canonicalisation algorithm
evidence_required: Existing C1 tests plus C4 vectors and release-candidate rerun

existing_capability: Provides digital-signature authenticity and integrity for exact bytes under a trusted public key
route_c_use: Authenticates the canonical TrustEvidence envelope in the reference implementation
route_c_profile_decision: Keep issuer-key role separate from backend-key role and verify the declared profile before accepting the statement
explicit_nonclaim: A valid signature does not prove clinical truth identity proofing legal non-repudiation or complete event capture
evidence_required: Existing C1 tests plus C4 substitution and key-role mutations

existing_capability: Binds concealed bytes when the verifier possesses the nonce and candidate payload
route_c_use: Commits to the source clinical payload without placing clinical values in the portable envelope
route_c_profile_decision: Declare exact input framing algorithm and nonce handling; keep the nonce outside the public envelope
explicit_nonclaim: Hashing is not encryption and a low-entropy input without an undisclosed nonce can leak information
evidence_required: Existing C1 tests plus C4 payload and nonce mutations and C3 payload-exclusion scan

existing_capability: Defines Certificate Transparency Merkle inclusion and consistency algorithms
route_c_use: Provides algorithmic lineage for local tree and proof reasoning where the implementation matches the cited operation
route_c_profile_decision: Document every local deviation and label the output project-specific
explicit_nonclaim: The local A2 object is not a Certificate Transparency log or a universally witnessed service
evidence_required: C4 vector-level implementation audit and mutations

existing_capability: Defines generic roles and architecture for single-issuer signed-statement transparency
route_c_use: Used only as a direct comparator and future interoperability direction
route_c_profile_decision: Keep the Route C envelope and local backend terminology separate from SCITT media types and conformance language
explicit_nonclaim: No SCITT implementation or conformance claim in Route C

existing_capability: Defines COSE_Sign1 receipts carrying verifiable-data-structure proofs including RFC9162_SHA256 inclusion and consistency
route_c_use: Used only to delimit what a standards-conformant receipt would require
route_c_profile_decision: Call the current object a project-specific local A2 Merkle receipt; do not use the RFC media type or protected-header semantics
explicit_nonclaim: No RFC 9942 implementation or conformance claim in Route C

existing_capability: No single cited standard prescribes the complete Route C portable referenced committed excluded decision for the frozen healthcare dispute
route_c_use: Defines the facts selected before the FHIR and cryptographic layers are applied
route_c_profile_decision: Tie every field to an accountability question and lower-exposure alternative; remove fields that do not survive necessity and privacy tests
explicit_nonclaim: No universal field set or mature healthcare standard is claimed
evidence_required: C3 hero case; field classification; field deletion; payload scan; reviewer traceability

existing_capability: No single cited profile establishes the exact one-to-one relationship between the Route C envelope and the selected FHIR artefacts
route_c_use: Makes the envelope inspectable in established healthcare structures while preserving exact signed bytes
route_c_profile_decision: Maintain one mapping registry and test semantic round trip separately from byte preservation
explicit_nonclaim: FHIR serialisation is not the signed source of truth and validation does not prove clinical validity
evidence_required: C3 mapping registry official validators round trip and Binary byte preservation

existing_capability: The existing project backend appends envelope commitments and returns a signed JSON receipt with proof material
route_c_use: Supplies bounded local backend evidence for mutation and cost experiments
route_c_profile_decision: Verify candidate entry proof root backend signature and prior checkpoint under one declared policy
explicit_nonclaim: Not a public transparency service not a COSE Receipt not complete not production persistent
evidence_required: C4 mutation corpus and C5 local B2 measurement
